Class entry

java.lang.Object
entry
All Implemented Interfaces:
Comparable<entry>
public class entry extends Object implements Comparable<entry>
Entry class to represent a word and how many times it has been repeated

    repeated

    Compares both words alphebetically

    Indicates whether some other object is "equal to" this one.

    Gets the number of times this word has been added

    getWord()
    Gets the word stored in this entry.

    repeated()
    Increments the repeat count for this word by one.

    Sets or updates the word stored in this entry.

    Returns a string representation of the entry.

  • Field Details

    • word

      private String word

    • repeated

      private int repeated

  • Constructor Details

    • entry

      public entry(String word)
      Constructs a new entry with the specified word. initializes the repeat count to 1 and converts the word to lowercase.
      Parameters:
      word - The string to be stored in this entry.

  • Method Details

    • getWord

      public String getWord()
      Gets the word stored in this entry.
      Returns:
      The word string.

    • setWord

      public void setWord(String word)
      Sets or updates the word stored in this entry.
      Parameters:
      word - The new word string.

    • getRepeated

      public int getRepeated()
      Gets the number of times this word has been added
      Returns:
      The repeat count.

    • repeated

      public void repeated()
      Increments the repeat count for this word by one.

    • compareTo

      public int compareTo(entry other)
      Compares both words alphebetically
      Specified by:
      compareTo in interface Comparable<entry>
      Parameters:
      other - The entry to be compared.
      Returns:
      A negative integer, zero, or a positive integer to show if the word comes before, is the same, or comes after the other word

    • equals

      public boolean equals(Object o)
      Indicates whether some other object is "equal to" this one. Two entries are considered equal if they contain the same word string.
      Overrides:
      equals in class Object
      Parameters:
      o - The reference object with which to compare.
      Returns:
      true if this object is the same as the obj argument or contains the same word; false otherwise.

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Returns a string representation of the entry.
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object
      Returns:
      A string describing the word and its repeat count.
